title: Process to prepare higher ethylene amines or urea derivatives thereof
abstract: The present invention relates to a to prepare ethylene amines of the formula NH2-(C2H4-NH-)pH wherein p is at least 2, or derivatives thereof wherein one or more units -NH-C2H4-NH- may be present as a cyclic ethylene urea unit (I) comprising the step of reacting an ethanolamine-functional compound OH-(C2H4-NH-)qH or HO-(C2H4-NH)q-C2H4-OH wherein q is at least 1, an amine-functional compound NH2-(C2H4-NH-)rH wherein r is at least 1 in the presence of a carbon oxide delivering agent and water, with a molar ratio of water : carbon oxide delivering agent of from 0.01:1 to 2:1.
claims: Claims Claims: 1 . Process to prepare ethylene amines of the formula NH 2 -(C2H -NH-) P H wherein p is at least 2, or derivatives thereof wherein one or more units - NH-C2H -NH- may be present as a cyclic ethylene urea unit comprising the step of reacting an ethanolamine-functional compound OH-(C 2 H 4 -NH-) q H or HO-(C 2 H 4 -NH) q -C 2 H 4 -OH wherein q is at least 1 , an amine-functional compound NH 2 -(C 2 H -NH-) r H wherein r is at least 1 in the presence of a carbon oxide delivering agent and water, with a molar ratio of water : carbon oxide delivering agent of from 0.01 :1 to 2:1 . 2. Process of claim 1 wherein the molar ratio of water : carbon oxide delivering agent is between 0.05:1 and 1 :1 . Process according to any one of the preceding claims 1 or 2, where the carbon oxide delivering agent is carbon dioxide, urea or provided at least in part as one compound with the ethanolamine-functional compound and/or the amine-functional compound in the form of a CO adduct, preferably an adduct comprising a cyclic ethylene urea unit a carbamate unit or a linear urea structure 4. Process according to any one of the preceding claims wherein in a preceding step carbon dioxide is reacted with an ethanolamine-functional compound or an amine-functional compound and the carbon oxide delivering agent is present in the process at least partly as a cyclic or non-cyclic carbamate derivative of the ethanolamine-functional compound or as a cyclic or non-cyclic urea derivative of the amine- functional compound, or a combination of these. Process according to claim 4 containing an additional step of removing water after the carbon dioxide has reacted with the ethanolamine- functional compound or the amine-functional compound. Process according to any one of the preceding claims wherein water is added or removed during the process intermittently, semi-continuously or continuously to maintain the amount of water such that the molar ratio of water : carbon oxide delivering agent remains from 0.01 :1 to 2:1 . Process according to any one of the preceding claims wherein the molar ratio of carbon oxide delivering agent : amine-functional compound is of from 0.1 :1 to 10:1 , preferably higher than 1 :1 up to 2.5:1 . Process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein amine- functional compound comprises an amino-functional compound of the formula HN-(CH2-CH 2 -NH) r -H, wherein r is 1 to 10, more in particular 1 to 5, preferably compounds selected from the group of ethylenediamine (EDA), diethylenetriamine (DETA), triethylenetetramine (TETA), tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), and mixtures thereof. Process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the ethanolamine-functional compound comprises a ethanolamine-functional compound of the formula ÎÎ-(ÎÎ 2 -ÎÎ 2 -ÎÎ) Ï -Î wherein q is 1 to 10, more in particular 1 to 5, preferably compounds selected from the group of monoethanolamine (MEA), aminoethylethanolamine (AEEA) and hydroxyethyl diethylene triamine (HE-DETA). 10. Process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the reaction product comprising one or more compounds in the form of urea adducts is subjected to a CO removal reaction to convert the urea adduct into amine compounds.
